title: Wire verified CF Access JWT as an alternate credential into AuthSession
action: In src/web/auth.rs, the AuthSession extractor (FromRequestParts) currently authenticates a request only via the hg_session cookie against state.sessions. Extend it so that when the AppState carries a configured cf_access_team_domain and cf_access_aud (from the cell-1 config fields), the extractor ALSO accepts a request as authenticated if it carries a Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion header whose value passes the cell-1 verification function (src/web/cf_access.rs) -- i.e. try the existing cookie check first (or either order), and if the cookie check fails, fall back to checking the CF Access header; only silent_404() if BOTH fail (or if CF Access isn't configured, keep exactly today's cookie-only behavior). Do not remove or weaken the existing cookie/session path in any way -- this is strictly additive. When CF Access auth succeeds, do not require or auto-mint a session cookie; each guarded request just re-verifies the header (the JWKS cache from cell 1 keeps this cheap). Thread whatever new config (team_domain/aud) is needed from AppState -- check how AppState is constructed in src/main.rs and src/web/mod.rs so the new optional config reaches AppState the same way web_secret does. Keep the change minimal and localized to the AuthSession extractor; do not touch the /api/login or /api/logout handlers themselves (CF Access users simply never need to POST /api/login -- they hit a guarded endpoint directly and the extractor recognizes their edge-verified identity).

truths: When cf_access_team_domain/cf_access_aud are unset, AuthSession behavior is byte-identical to before this cell (cookie-only, silent 404 on failure) -- this must be provably true, not assumed
When configured and a request carries a valid Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion header (per cell 1's verification), a guarded endpoint succeeds even with no hg_session cookie present
When configured and the header is missing, invalid, wrong aud, or wrong iss, the request still gets exactly today's silent_404 -- no new error shape, no information disclosure
The existing static-token + cookie login path (/api/login, /api/logout) is untouched and still works exactly as before

artifacts.substantive: AuthSession::from_request_parts accepts a verified CF Access JWT as an alternate credential alongside the session cookie, config-gated

prohibitions: Do not remove, weaken, or bypass the existing hg_session cookie check
Do not change /api/login or /api/logout handler behavior
Do not trust the Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion header without calling cell 1's full JWKS/signature/aud/iss verification
Do not change the tailnet-only default transport or any bind-address logic

verify: cargo build --quiet 2>&1 | tail -80 && cargo test --quiet 2>&1 | tail -100 && cargo fmt --all --check 2>&1 | tail -40 && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tail -80

trace.outcome: AuthSession accepts a verified Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion header as an alternate credential when CF Access is configured; cookie path and unconfigured behavior unchanged
